Module 5:
Nail the Hookpoint
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Time to Nail the Hookpoint
What is the hookpoint?
A singular moment in time when your audience is having an IMMERSIVE experience...
... and they start ADDING VALUE to the deal.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
How the Buyer Adds Value
How does the buyer add value, and how do you recognize that has happened?
It’s when they start contributing ways to make...
The deal stronger
The relationship better
The transaction easier.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Real-World Hookpoint Examples
Look for phrases such as:
1. “We’re really excited to get the software installed, hey we could introduce you to our suppliers who
also need this software.”
2. “Don’t worry about getting approval from our Chief Financial Officer, we’ll help make that happen so
you don’t have to deal with it.”
3. “Normally we can only invest $1M in any deal outside of Europe, but we’re going to break the rules
here and raise our investment to $3M if you’ll agree.”
4. “Let’s spend some social time together, we’d like to take you out to dinner…”
5. “If you can close in two weeks, we’ll pay the legal costs to help make this happen.”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Types of Value-Adds
These kinds of Value-Adds can be thought of as any action the Buyer takes to...
- Sell themselves the deal (doing your job for you)
- Negotiate against themselves
- Concede important deal points for no benefit
- Sell themselves to you unnecessarily
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
This is the Nature of Pitching
Where does this concept of Value Add come from?
Imagine you are pitching a deal...
What are you trying to do?
Get attention...
Tell a great story...
Keep it intriguing...
Time constrain...
Provide fast, visual stimuli...
Frame control … yes yes and yes.
But ultimately, you’re trying to ADD VALUE.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Value Exchange Drives Human Interaction
You’ve heard this many times in many ways during your career:
What’s your value add?
How do those guys add value?
How is this additive?
You do something for me, I’ll give you money or do something for you. It’s ALL value exchange.
If you have nothing to exchange for value, you’re dead.
VALUE FLOWS BACK AND FORTH IN ALL RELATIONSHIPS. It’s the way of things.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Personal Relationships Are Reciprocal
With people you know, value is reciprocal.
I cook, you do the dishes. I change the copier toner, you take checks to the bank, etc.
Stop reciprocating, and people stop liking you very quickly.
Why?
Because you are TAKING value without adding it in return.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Business Relationships Are Different
With people you don’t know, there’s isn’t such a strong reciprocal relationship.
There may not be one at all.
You have to add almost ALL the value.
You are pitching them, seeking that they do something they weren’t otherwise planning to do.
Therefore, you need to the add value.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
The Value Tipping Point
Buyer reaches a point inside themselves that says,
“I’ve taken a lot of value here. I’ve received a lot. These guys are great. They care. It’s time for me to
reciprocate, it’s time to add some value to this relationship.”
At the MOMENT your buyer gets this gut sense to contribute, YOU HAVE NAILED THE HOOKPOINT.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Two Ingredients for the Hookpoint
You have nailed the hookpoint when two things are present:
1. The audience/buyer has started to add value.
2. This happens while they are IMMERSED in your presentation.
Now, let’s talk about immersion for a minute...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Immersion Defined
Immersion
A state of focus so intense that awareness of the outside world is lost, resulting in a feeling of
happiness, joy, comfort and satisfaction.
You can think of it as social flow.
Another way to think about this in a business context is CHEMISTRY.
When you just “hit it off,” body language is great.
You’re syncopated, the gears are meshed, you’re completing each other’s sentences.
Advertising agencies know this well.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Additional Examples
Look for the audience to add real value, to negotiate against themselves, to lean in, suggest ways for
The relationship to get stronger
“We can fly out to meet you!”
The transaction to get easier
“Look, let’s not wait for legal to sort all this out, we’ll send a check today to get the transaction
started.”
The terms to get better
“Let’s not argue about price. Just make it $30M and let’s move on.”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Immersion Located
Where do you find immersion?
At the crossroads of experiencing and thinking.
At the center of feeling and analyzing.
At the intersection of Perception and Cognition...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
How Do You Get Immersion?
First you need to understand two things.
Perceptual systems and cognitive systems
Perceptual systems
What we take in through our senses.
Cognitive systems
What we think with that information.
Let’s dig into these more deeply...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Finding the Sweet Spot
When these systems are under-taxed, the result is apathy and boredom.
When over-taxed, the result is stress and frustration.
Immersion happens when perceptual and cognitive systems are challenged at near capacity, without
being exceeded.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Characteristics of Immersion
Immersion is characterized by:
· 	Challenges that can be overcome
· 	A feeling of control over situation where they are learning interesting things
· 	A loss of concern over matters of the self (awareness of hunger, thirst)
· 	A modified sense of time (hours pass, it feels like minutes)
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Immersion and Wanting
Theme park rides: Loaded on the perceptual side, no cognitive stimulation.
Chess: Completely cognitive, no perceptual stimulation.
The optimal immersive experience involve rich sensory experiences and rich cognitive engagement.
- challenges that can be overcome
- environments that minimize distractions
- promote a feeling of control
- emphasize stimuli that are warm vs. cold (examples)
- disconnect from reality and build the future
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
You will need to have hit certain “waypoint” during the lead up to the hookpoint.
Traditional waypoints — sets of coordinates that identify a point in physical space.
Today, used for navigation, increasingly abstract.
They define invisible routing paths for navigation.
Waypoint
A fixed point along a planned course used to make a journey, or simply a point of reference useful for
navigation.
This applies to pitching as well...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
THE BIG IDEA: Tell people in the most basic terms what is interesting, why this is important and if
everything works perfectly - what can be achieved:
1. DID YOU KNOW ...
2. SO?
3. SOMETHING MAJOR HAPPENS.
4. ECONOMICALLY IMPORTANT.
5. IF YOU CAN FIX THIS PROBLEM ...
6. YOU CAN GET A HUGE BENEFIT.
7. THIS DOCUMENT IS ABOUT HOW TO...
When you get a wound you expose the inside of your body.
So what?
Germs flood in! That’s always bad news - it leads to serious infection.
This costs hospitals $100B/year.
If you could even slightly lower rates of hospital infections ...
You could save lives and make money.
Reduce hospital acquired infections and grow a $50M company
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
PRIZE AFTER “T”(TELL THE STORY):
I’M GLAD WE had some time to spend with you today. As you can see from the presentation I just gave,
this is the best WIDGET in the world. We have more people who want our widgets than I can deal with,
and I’m choosing my customers carefully.
Tell me about yourself. How do I know you’re a good customer.
Why should I pick you as my next customer?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
Don’t talk about playing golf. Or the time you visited XYZ City where the buyer is from.
Don’t compare schools you attended, or who you both know at Microsoft. Don’t talk about your
enthusiasm/interests, or theirs. Don’t wax philsophical about other experiences that were fun.
Have Fun in This Meeting Right NOW. In the immediate moment. This should BE FUN. It’s not talking
about how other things were fun on a different day in a different place.
Engage socially. Have fun. Make it fun and interesting and compelling. If you aren’t having a good time,
how the hell are they going to? Engage socially and make it fun.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
Perpetrate a denial or defiance:
Buyer: I’d like to talk to some customers as references.
You: No. I’m not going to waste the time of my best customers on this. They’re busy making money.
When I see that you’re really ready to commit, then I’ll think about who I can let you talk to.
Remember all the frames you can use: Intrigue Frame, Prize Frame, Power Frame, Time Frame, Moral
Authority Frame & Money-is-a-Commodity Frame.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
You: I’m sensing there’s a real possibility this is not going to work out. I might not be willing so sell you
this widget. You seem like you’d be a difficult customer over the long term.
long pause.
But then again, you’re a well respected company. Maybe I should take some extra time to understand
you more ....
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
As you can see, things are great now. But it wasn’t always this way.
When I started selling these widgets, we only had Version 1.0.
There weren’t many buyers. But I stuck with it.
My friends told me to quit. And I thought about it more than once.
But in the end, I stuck it out, through the hard times. Now - it’s quite different.
We’re at version 5.0, the best in the market. Customers come to me. I control pricing. It was a long
difficult road, and the temptation to quit was always there. But I persisted, and it has paid off Big Time.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
Introduce new ideas, new concepts, new thinking.
Why bother?
If the audience can guess what you’re going to say next, or the points you are making are obvious, or
you aren’t providing new insight ...
Then they are going to stop paying attention.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
For [target customers]
who are dissatisfied with [the current products in the market],
My Idea/product is a [a new product category]
that provides [key problems/solutions features].
Unlike [the competing product]
my idea/product has [key features].
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
Introduce the problem your product solves:
Then admit there’s a GENERIC SOLUTION;
admit there are ways that the problem can
be solved without you
Then introduce your special (and better
solution)
Insects are difficult to deter from crops in the
summer months...
Today you can solve this problem with
insecticide, genetically modified-seeds or even
by accepting high loss rates...
But our Blue Plastic Frogs are the best wide-
range insect deterrent in the world ...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
AFTER delivering the pitch for :20 minutes, your status should be high.
Make sure to apply counterpressure: “What kind of customer/buyer are you ...
would we enjoy working with you ... and would we profit?”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
Asking your buyers questions will annoy them. Sure, they sales books tell you to ask lots of questions.
That’s a relic of 90’s style sales techniques. QUESTIONS ARE ANNOYING.
Instead, make statements that draw your buyer or audience into a conversation.
Instead of this: What are your sales goals for 2012? How many employees will you add in the year?
What new products are you introducing? How many shipments do you do a month?
Try this: Look’s like there’s growth in your business this year. If we’re going to help you out, we should
talk more about your product mix.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 31 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
Neediness of any sort kills deals. Eradicate it:
1. Want nothing.
2. Seek no validation from others
3. Do your best
4. Announce your intention to leave. Then do it.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 32 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Waypoints
START OF MEETING
You: Let’s get started here. I have about :20 minute to pitch you our idea, then there’s some time for
Q&A. I’ll let you tell me a little about yourselves, then I’ve got to get going to another engagement.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 33 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
A Quick Lesson from Navy SEALs
Trained to look for specific results from an action.
If they kick someone in the groin: arms fling out. That’s the physical reaction to being kicked there.
If they break someone’s ankle: both hands grab the ankle and target doubles over.
If they don’t see that reaction – they know they didn’t get the RESULT.
You too will be looking for the results of your actions...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 34 of 37 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Results to Look For
1. The big idea ... 	SUDDEN ATTENTION, NODDING APPRECIATIVELY
ACTION 	RESULT
2. Prizing
3. Fun social engagement
4. Frame control
5. Tension
6. Build a character
7. Mix novelty and intrigue
8. Clear value proposition
9. Generic solution
14. Time constraint
13. Willingness to walk away
12. Made statements
11. Applied counterpressure
10. Croc friendly pitch
INNER SENSE OF CONTROL
(NONE)
INTERNAL
SMILING FROM AUDIENCE, BODY LANGUAGE
RECAPTURING OF ATTENTION
THEY ARE REACTING TO YOU
INTERNAL
VERBAL EXCHANGES
TENSION, QUALIFYING & PROOFING
ATTENTION CUES
AUDIENCE RELAXES, CALMS
SELF APPRECIATION
ATTENTION
www.getwsodownload.com

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
A Critical Step
This is a critical step in the pitch.
It cannot be overlooked.
When executed properly, you’re on your way to...
Getting the Deal
www.getwsodownload.com
